name: Pita Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine 2 cups flour, sugar, salt and yeast in a large bowl.
2. Heat water and oil in a small saucepan until very warm (120 to 130F).
3. Add the warm liquid to the flour mixture.
4. Blend at low speed until moistened.
5. Beat for 3 minutes at medium speed.
6. Stir in an additional 2-1/2 to 3 cups flour by hand until the dough pulls cleanly away from the sides of the bowl.
7. Place dough in a greased bowl, cover loosely with plastic wrap and cloth towel.
8. Let rise in a warm place until light and doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 hours.
9. Punch down dough, divide into 2 parts, then mold into balls.
10. Allow to rest on a floured surface, covered with an inverted bowl, for 15 minutes.
11. Divide dough into 14 equal pieces and shape into balls.
12. On a lightly floured surface, roll each ball into a 7-inch circle about 1/8-inch thick.
13. Place circles about 2 inches apart on cornmeal-sprinkled baking sheets.
14. Cover and let rise in a warm place for about 30 minutes.
15. Bake at 450F for 8 to 10 minutes, or until edges turn light golden brown.
